title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Miss Blacklock and Dora Bunner discuss a newspaper notice regarding a murder scheduled to take place in the park at six-thirty that evening. While Miss Bunner finds the notice frightening and dangerous, Miss Blacklock initially dismisses it as a joke. Their conversation is interrupted by Miki, a moody household helper and refugee from Europe. Miki, claiming her family was killed by enemies, expresses a desperate desire to leave because she believes Nazis or Bolsheviks are coming to kill her. She cites the newspaper notice as proof of the impending danger. Miss Blacklock attempts to calm her by suggesting they cook beef and cheese straws, noting that people will be arriving at six-thirty for a "funeral."

Later, in the drawing room of the Victorian-style house, Miss Blacklock prepares for the arrival of guests. She has arranged flowers, a tablecloth, and drinks. Patrick and Julia join her, leading to a discussion about the scarcity of coal and the differences between the past and the present. Miss Bunner recalls their shared history, mentioning that Letty was once a highly educated secretary. Felicia Hyms enters the room, surprised to find a gathering. Patrick dramatically announces that the room is a "crime scene," explaining that the flowers are wreaths and the food is for a funeral feast. When Felicia and Philippa Hammers express confusion and ask if it is a joke, Miss Blacklock instructs them to show Felicia the notice before she goes to shut up the ducks.
